sourceTitle: Unleashing productivity with Notion's Slack integration

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of integrating Slack with Notion?

options.text: It reduces the constant back‑and‑forth between the two apps, letting you stay focused.
options.explanation: The guide states that the integration cuts out the back‑and‑forth, boosting productivity.

options.text: It increases the number of Slack messages you receive each day.
options.explanation: The integration does not affect message volume; it streamlines information flow.

options.text: It replaces Notion entirely with Slack for all documentation.
options.explanation: Notion remains the place for storing and organizing information; Slack is only for real‑time chat.

options.text: It removes the need for any notifications in either tool.
options.explanation: Notifications are actually a key feature of the integration, not something eliminated.

questions.question: Where do you enable private Slack notifications for mentions in Notion?

options.text: In Notion’s side panel under Settings → My Notifications & Settings.
options.explanation: The article instructs users to go to Settings then My Notifications & Settings to turn on Slack notifications.

options.text: In the database’s Automations tab.
options.explanation: Automations are for channel‑wide notifications, not private ones.

options.text: In the Slack workspace admin console.
options.explanation: Slack admin settings control Slack itself, not Notion‑specific notification preferences.

options.text: In the Notion template gallery.
options.explanation: Templates are for pre‑built page structures, not notification settings.

questions.question: Which feature lets you reply to a Notion comment directly from Slack?

options.text: Click the **Reply** button on the Slack notification message.
options.explanation: The guide mentions a ‘Reply’ action in Slack that posts back to Notion as a regular comment.

options.text: Use Notion AI to generate a response.
options.explanation: Notion AI can summarize, but replying directly from Slack uses the Reply button, not AI.

options.text: Create a new task in Notion from the Slack message.
options.explanation: Creating a task is a different workflow; it doesn’t post a comment reply.

options.text: Share the Notion page link again in Slack.
options.explanation: Resharing a link does not send a comment back to Notion.

questions.question: To receive a Slack notification when a task’s status changes to “complete”, which sequence of actions is correct?

options.text: Open the database → Automations → Add trigger → Choose the **Status** property → Select only “complete” → Add action → Choose Slack notification → Pick channel → Create.
options.explanation: These are the exact steps outlined in the guide for setting up a status‑change trigger.

options.text: Open the database → Settings → My Notifications → Enable Slack for all status changes.
options.explanation: My Notifications handles private mentions, not database‑level status triggers.

options.text: In Slack, add a custom slash command that posts to Notion when you type /complete.
options.explanation: Slash commands are not part of the described integration workflow.

options.text: Create a new Notion page titled “Complete Tasks” and share its link in Slack.
options.explanation: Sharing a page does not automatically generate status‑change notifications.

questions.question: How can you capture a Slack message as a task in Notion?

options.text: Select **More Actions** on the Slack message and choose **Create task in Notion**.
options.explanation: The guide describes this exact flow to turn a Slack message into a Notion task.

options.text: Copy the message text, paste it into Notion, and manually add task properties.
options.explanation: While possible, this is not the shortcut the integration provides.

options.text: Ask Notion AI to generate a task from the Slack conversation.
options.explanation: Notion AI can summarize, but the direct creation uses the More Actions menu.

options.text: Send the message to a dedicated email address that feeds Notion.
options.explanation: Email ingestion is not mentioned as part of the Slack‑Notion integration.

questions.question: When you share a Notion page link in Slack, what does the link preview display?

options.text: The page’s title and the creator’s name.
options.explanation: The article states that Slack unfurls the link to show the title and creator, giving context at a glance.

options.text: The full content of the Notion page.
options.explanation: Only a summary (title and creator) is shown, not the entire page.

options.text: Only the raw URL with no additional information.
options.explanation: Slack provides a richer preview, not just the URL.

options.text: A list of all databases referenced on the page.
options.explanation: Database listings are not part of the preview shown in Slack.

questions.question: What permissions are required to connect Slack to Notion AI connectors?

options.text: You must be a Notion workspace owner **and** a Slack workspace admin (or an approved Slack member).
options.explanation: The guide explicitly lists these two roles as prerequisites for using Notion AI connectors with Slack.

options.text: Any Notion member can connect Slack to Notion AI.
options.explanation: Only owners/admins have the authority to set up the connector.

options.text: Only the Slack workspace admin is needed; Notion permissions are irrelevant.
options.explanation: Both Notion and Slack permissions are required, not just Slack.

options.text: No special permissions are needed; the integration works for all users by default.
options.explanation: The integration has explicit permission requirements.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a use case for the Slack‑Notion integration?

options.text: Generating invoices automatically from Slack conversations.
options.explanation: The guide never mentions invoicing; it focuses on tasks, feedback, bugs, and context sharing.

options.text: Capturing customer feedback from Slack into a Notion database.
options.explanation: Customer feedback capture is explicitly described as a use case.

options.text: Sending private Slack notifications when you are mentioned in Notion.
options.explanation: Private mention notifications are a featured capability.

options.text: Providing richer context via link previews between the two tools.
options.explanation: Link previews are highlighted as a key benefit.

questions.question: When adding multiple triggers to a Notion database automation for Slack notifications, which statement is true?

options.text: You can add unlimited triggers by clicking the plus sign and selecting additional property changes.
options.explanation: The guide’s Pro Tip says you can add as many triggers as you want using the plus sign.

options.text: Only one trigger per automation is allowed; adding more will overwrite the first.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states you can add multiple triggers, not just one.

options.text: All triggers must monitor the same property type (e.g., only Status properties).
options.explanation: Triggers can monitor different properties; the guide does not impose a single‑property restriction.

options.text: Adding more than two triggers disables the Slack notification action.
options.explanation: There is no limitation on the number of triggers affecting the action; more triggers simply expand coverage.

questions.question: How does Notion AI leverage Slack context to provide insights, and what limitation applies to its usage?

options.text: Notion AI searches both Notion pages and Slack messages to answer questions, but it only works for workspace owners and Slack admins (or approved members).
options.explanation: The guide explains that AI pulls information from both sources and requires owner/admin permissions.

options.text: Notion AI only reads Slack messages; it cannot access Notion content.
options.explanation: AI is designed to combine information from both platforms, not just Slack.

options.text: Anyone in the Slack channel can ask Notion AI questions without any permission checks.
options.explanation: Permissions are required; not every user can invoke the connector.

options.text: Notion AI can automatically create new Slack channels based on Notion project updates.
options.explanation: Creating channels is not a described capability of Notion AI connectors.
